title: Cervical spondylosis - NHS
text:
cervical spondylosis as part of getting older.
It happens when ageing causes wear and tear to bones in the spine and the soft cushions of tissue (discs) in the neck.
You may also be more likely to get cervical spondylosis at any age if:
you have previously had a neck injury you have a family history of the condition 
Page last reviewed: 02 August 2023
Next review due: 02 August 2026
